mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero indicates extremely accommodative monetary policy, which compresses discount rates and supports elevated equity valuations. This accommodative stance is sustained and always positive for equities until rates rise significantly.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% in June 2009 is at the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or the 20-day forward return after a rate hike (FEDFUNDS > 0.5%) does not become negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the unemployment rate from elevated post-crisis levels signals labor market tightening, rising household incomes, and improved consumer sentiment, which drives consumption and corporate earnings higher over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.5 percentage points over a rolling 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE peaked at 10.0% in October 2009 and is 9.4% in June 2009, likely to decline
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return following a UNRATE decline of 0.5pp is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) compared to other periods, or the direction is negative in more than 40% of trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero interest rates and an uptick in retail sales signals that monetary stimulus is translating into consumer spending, creating a positive feedback loop for corporate profits and equity prices. Both conditions must hold simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:In May 2009 RSAFS was 339993 (revised 327011), still low but stabilizing
regime_hint: early expansion with zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average 20-day return, or the Rank IC for the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During recessionary periods (defined by elevated unemployment), rising consumer price inflation represents cost-push pressures that squeeze corporate margins and reduce real earnings, leading to negative equity returns. In expansions the effect reverses but this hypothesis applies only to the recession regime.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.4% in June 2009 indicates recessionary conditions
regime_hint: recession regime defined by UNRATE > 8% or GDPC1 negative year-over-year
failure_condition: In months when UNRATE > 8%, the correlation between CPIAUCSL change and forward 20-day return is not negative (rank correlation >= 0), or the mean return during inflation rises is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production is a coincident indicator of real economic activity. A sustained recovery in INDPRO from its cyclical trough signals broad-based gains in manufacturing, which feeds into employment and corporate earnings, providing a positive trend signal over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO trough was around 85.9 in April 2009, likely to rise
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not positively correlated with 3-month changes in INDPRO (Spearman rho < 0.1), or the strategy of buying when INDPRO rises underperforms the market over any 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in retail sales surprises markets and signals an abrupt pullback in consumer spending, which is a major component of GDP. Such shocks often lead to immediate downward revisions in earnings expectations and a negative short-term equity reaction.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS in May 2009 was 339993, still below pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return after a >1% decline in RSAFS is not negative or is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are rising and the unemployment rate is declining simultaneously, it indicates a self-reinforcing cycle of construction activity, job creation, and consumer confidence. This joint improvement is a powerful signal for sustained economic expansion and rising equity prices.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% and UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.1 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:In April 2009 HOUST was 458 (revised 478), very low but showing signs of bottom
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following the joint trigger is not significantly higher than the unconditional mean, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate testing over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In the zero-lower-bound environment (fed funds rate below 1%), housing starts become a particularly sensitive leading indicator of economic momentum because construction activity directly stimulates employment and consumption. Rising housing starts during ZIRP strongly predict positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% in June 2009 confirms zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: In months when FEDFUNDS < 1%, the correlation between HOUST change and forward 20-day return is not positive (rho < 0), or the average return following positive HOUST months is not greater than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
